page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m8-vws.2xlarge is a KVM-hypervised virtual server belonging to the ecs.vgn6i-vws family. It features 10 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 46.0 GB of system memory. The instance is equipped with a fractional GPU configuration consisting of a 0.5x NVIDIA T4 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM. It does not include local storage, requiring external storage attachments, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance is weak, ranking in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and bottom 10% for Geekbench single-core tests. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth read/write operations fall into the average tier. Cryptographic and database operations, such as Redis SET and Passmark database tests, also demonstrate average performance. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference speeds are average for small models (135M) but drop to poor (bottom 10%) for medium models (7B).

The primary tradeoff of this instance is its fractional GPU allocation and weak single-core CPU execution, which limits its utility for single-threaded tasks or heavy LLM inference. However, the 0.5x NVIDIA T4 GPU configuration offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads requiring GPU acceleration without the expense of a full dedicated GPU. The lack of local storage means storage costs and performance will depend entirely on attached network volumes.

This server is configured for entry-level GPU-accelerated tasks, lightweight machine learning inference with small models, ray tracing, image processing, and general-purpose multi-threaded applications that benefit from fractional GPU resources and moderate memory capacity.

It lacks local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark data reveals weak single-core CPU performance, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and bottom 10% for Geekbench single-core tests. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations score in the average range. The fractional GPU design offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that require GPU acceleration but do not demand a full dedicated card. This instance is suited for lightweight machine learning inference, image processing, ray tracing, and general-purpose multi-threaded applications.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining ten dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with a fractional NVIDIA T4 GPU for entry-level accelerated computing.
bullet_points: 10 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs and 46.0 GB of system memory.
Fractional 0.5x NVIDIA T4 GPU with 8 GB of dedicated VRAM.
No local storage with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.
Average multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance.
Suited for lightweight machine learning inference, image processing, and ray tracing.
